name: chains
description: Crossing a circled chain space auto-crosses its linked partner, even out of left-to-right order on the partner's row and even into an already-locked partner row; an already-marked partner is left alone (no double mark). The forced co-mark never locks a row by itself.

steps.note: red[4] (red 6) is chained to yellow[4] (yellow 6); marking it auto-crosses yellow 6 too, as ONE action
yellow[4] is already marked (via the chain, not directly) - marking it again directly is illegal

yellow[1] (yellow 3, chained to green[1]/green 11) is now forfeited: the auto co-mark set yellow's maxMarkedIndex to 4, so index 1 < 4 is illegal exactly like any ordinary forfeited cell - the chain grants no left-to-right immunity on its OWN row
close blue for no points, locking it without touching its marks - sets up the next check

green[8] (green 4) is chained to blue[8] (blue 4). Marking green[8] is legal (green untouched so far) and auto-crosses blue[8] EVEN THOUGH blue is already locked - the automatic co-mark ignores the normal marking rules entirely, per the official rule
undo reverses the deliberate green[8] cross AND its forced blue[8] co-mark together, as one atomic step - blue stays locked (concede is a separate history entry)
redo re-applies markColor(green,8) through the same mutator, which re-derives the chain partner deterministically and reproduces the identical auto co-mark
now 2 rows are locked (blue, yellow) - the game ends
once the game is over, every mutation's precondition is false, including a legal-looking chain cross
